title: Pyrus poilanei
intro: Pyrus poilanei, also known as Poilane's pear, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to Laos and Vietnam. The health benefits of Pyrus poilanei include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pyrus poilanei is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Pyrus poilanei is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the family Rosaceae. This species belongs to the genus Pyrus, commonly known as pear trees. Within the genus Pyrus, Pyrus poilanei is a distinct species. The classification further breaks down into the following ranks: kingdom (Plantae), phylum (Angiospermophyta), class (Rosopsida), order (Rosales), family (Rosaceae), genus (Pyrus), and species (P. poilanei).
